english: All this is pervaded by the Lord; possession loosens into stewardship.
spanish: Todo esto está penetrado por lo divino; la posesión se afloja en custodia.
recommendedEditionSource: F. Max Müller / Sacred Books of the East — Īśa Upanishad
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Upanishads witness; public-domain base translation anchor. Short working rendering.

english: Do not grasp at another's wealth; use only what belongs to the path.
spanish: No te aferres a la riqueza ajena; usa sólo lo que pertenece al camino.
recommendedEditionSource: F. Max Müller / Sacred Books of the East — Īśa Upanishad
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Upanishads witness, Īśa section. Short working rendering.

english: The still one outruns the mind before the senses can arrive.
spanish: Lo inmóvil adelanta a la mente antes de que los sentidos lleguen.
recommendedEditionSource: F. Max Müller / Sacred Books of the East — Īśa Upanishad
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Upanishads witness, Īśa section. Short working rendering.

english: It moves and does not move; it is far away and already here.
spanish: Se mueve y no se mueve; está lejos y ya está aquí.
recommendedEditionSource: F. Max Müller / Sacred Books of the East — Īśa Upanishad
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Upanishads witness, Īśa section. Short working rendering.

artifact: Īśa Upanishad — Self in All Beings
english: Who sees all beings in the Self no longer turns away from the world.
spanish: Quien ve todos los seres en el Sí mismo ya no se aparta del mundo.
recommendedEditionSource: F. Max Müller / Sacred Books of the East — Īśa Upanishad
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Upanishads witness, Īśa section. Short working rendering.

artifact: Kena Upanishad — Ear of the Ear
english: The source of hearing is not another sound; it is the ear of the ear.
spanish: La fuente del oír no es otro sonido; es el oído del oído.
recommendedEditionSource: F. Max Müller / Sacred Books of the East — Kena Upanishad
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Upanishads witness, Kena section. Short working rendering.

artifact: Kena Upanishad — Known by Not Knowing
original: यस्यामतं तस्य मतं मतं यस्य न वेद सः
english: The one who thinks it is grasped has missed it; the one who knows the limit begins to know.
spanish: Quien cree haberlo captado lo perdió; quien conoce el límite empieza a conocer.
recommendedEditionSource: F. Max Müller / Sacred Books of the East — Kena Upanishad
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Upanishads witness, Kena section. Short working rendering.

english: The good and the merely pleasant approach together; wisdom is choosing which one to follow.
spanish: Lo bueno y lo meramente agradable llegan juntos; la sabiduría es elegir a cuál seguir.
recommendedEditionSource: F. Max Müller / Sacred Books of the East — Katha Upanishad
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Upanishads witness, Katha section. Short working rendering.

english: The path is sharp as a razor; care itself becomes the road.
spanish: El camino es filoso como una navaja; el cuidado mismo se vuelve camino.
recommendedEditionSource: F. Max Müller / Sacred Books of the East — Katha Upanishad
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Upanishads witness, Katha section. Short working rendering.

english: That thou art: the teaching collapses distance into identity.
spanish: Eso eres tú: la enseñanza colapsa la distancia en identidad.
recommendedEditionSource: F. Max Müller / Sacred Books of the East — Chāndogya Upanishad
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Upanishads witness, Chāndogya section. Short working rendering.
